title: CBT for Adolescent Depression
abstract: We conducted a randomised controlled trial of cognitive behavioural therapy (CBT) in 312 adolescents aged 12-17 diagnosed with major depressive disorder. Participants received 16 sessions of CBT over 20 weeks. Primary outcome was change in CDRS-R score. CBT produced a mean CDRS-R reduction of 18.4 points versus 6.1 in the waitlist control (p<0.001). Remission was achieved in 46% of CBT participants. The intervention was safe with no serious adverse events.

title: Economic Impact of Global Chip Shortage on Semiconductor Supply Chain
abstract: This paper analyses the economic impact of the 2020-2022 global semiconductor shortage on automotive and consumer electronics supply chains. Using input-output modelling, we estimate GDP losses of $500 billion across G20 economies. Policy recommendations include diversified sourcing strategies and government investment in domestic fab capacity.

title: Vitamin D and Respiratory Infections
abstract: An individual patient data meta-analysis of 25 RCTs (n=11,321) testing vitamin D supplementation against acute respiratory tract infections. Supplementation significantly reduced infection risk overall (aOR 0.88). Benefit was largest in participants with baseline 25-hydroxyvitamin D below 25 nmol/L. Daily or weekly dosing was more effective than bolus dosing.

title: Machine Learning for Stock Price Prediction
abstract: We apply long short-term memory (LSTM) neural networks to predict daily closing prices of S&P 500 stocks. Training on 10 years of historical data with technical indicators, the model achieves a mean absolute percentage error of 2.3%. Backtesting shows a simulated annualised return of 14% versus a buy-and-hold benchmark of 11%.

title: Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction for Nurse Burnout
abstract: A cluster RCT in 24 hospital wards (n=412 nurses) compared an 8-week MBSR programme to usual care. MBSR significantly reduced emotional exhaustion (MBI subscale) by 6.8 points versus 1.2 in controls (95% CI 4.1-9.5). No significant effect on absenteeism. Attrition was 14% in the intervention arm.

title: Social Prescribing for Mild Depression in Primary Care
abstract: A pragmatic RCT tested social prescribing (linking patients to community activities) versus usual care in 1,824 adults with mild-to-moderate depression or anxiety across 42 GP practices. No significant difference was found in the primary composite outcome (GAD-7 + PHQ-9) at 6 months. Significant improvements were noted in social connectedness and WHO-5 wellbeing.

title: AI for Diabetic Retinopathy Screening
abstract: This retrospective analysis evaluated an AI algorithm (IDx-DR) on 248,390 retinal images from 84,312 adults with diabetes. Overall sensitivity was 88.4% and specificity 92.1%. Performance was significantly lower in Asian and Black patients. Image quality failure rates were higher in older patients.

title: Effects of Background Music Genre on Office Worker Productivity
abstract: A within-subjects experiment with 60 office workers tested the effects of classical, jazz, pop, and silence conditions on coding task performance and self-reported focus. Results showed no statistically significant differences in task completion time or error rate across conditions, though subjective preference for classical music was highest.

title: Metformin and All-Cause Mortality in Type 2 Diabetes
abstract: A retrospective cohort study (n=48,316) using UK Biobank linked primary care and hospital records examined metformin use and all-cause mortality over a median 7.4-year follow-up. After multivariable adjustment, metformin was associated with 24% lower mortality risk (HR 0.76, 95% CI 0.71-0.82). Results were consistent across age, sex, and eGFR subgroups.

title: Blockchain for Agricultural Supply Chain Transparency
abstract: We present a proof-of-concept blockchain system for tracking food products from farm to consumer. A pilot with 12 mango farms in Ghana demonstrated 98% scan compliance and full traceability within 48 hours of farm entry. Smart contracts automatically triggered payment upon delivery confirmation, reducing transaction time by 60%.

title: Community Health Worker Hypertension Programmes in Sub-Saharan Africa
abstract: A systematic review of 16 studies (10 RCTs) of CHW interventions for hypertension control in sub-Saharan Africa found significantly improved blood pressure control (OR 2.12, 95% CI 1.68-2.67) and medication adherence. Effects were larger in urban than rural settings. Most studies were pilots with short follow-up.

title: Gamification of University Physics Education
abstract: This quasi-experimental study compared a gamified physics curriculum (leaderboards, badges, quest-based problem sets) against traditional lectures in 230 first-year engineering students. Gamified students showed higher engagement scores but no significant difference in final exam performance. Intrinsic motivation increased in high-performing students only.

title: Long-COVID Symptom Trajectories: 18-Month Follow-Up
abstract: A prospective cohort of 3,241 adults with confirmed COVID-19 was followed for 18 months. Latent class analysis identified four trajectories: rapid recovery (52%), persistent fatigue-dominant (24%), fluctuating multi-symptom (16%), and progressive worsening (8%). Vaccination was associated with lower odds of persistent class membership (OR 0.58).

title: Urban Green Space and Real Estate Values
abstract: Using hedonic pricing models on 125,000 property transactions in London (2010-2020), we estimate that proximity to parks within 400 metres adds an average of 4.2% to residential property values. Effects were stronger in lower-income boroughs, suggesting green space investment may partially counteract spatial inequality in housing markets.

title: Probiotic Supplementation for Childhood Acute Gastroenteritis
abstract: An IPD meta-analysis of 22 RCTs (n=4,412 children aged 3 months-6 years) found no significant reduction in diarrhoea duration (-0.1 days, 95% CI -0.4 to 0.2) or hospitalisation from probiotic supplementation. Lactobacillus GG showed a marginal effect that did not survive multiple testing correction.

title: Ketamine for Treatment-Resistant Depression
abstract: A network meta-analysis of 38 RCTs (n=2,891) comparing ketamine formulations in treatment-resistant depression. IV ketamine showed highest response at 24 hours (OR 7.4). Intranasal esketamine maintained effect to 7 days (OR 3.1). Dissociative side effects were most common with IV route.

title: Impact of Minimum Wage Increases on Small Business Employment
abstract: This study uses difference-in-differences estimation across 50 US states from 2010-2022 to analyse the employment effects of minimum wage increases. Results suggest minimal negative employment effects in high-wage sectors but significant reductions (1-3% per $1 increase) in restaurant and retail employment, particularly for teenage workers.

title: Telemedicine vs In-Person Physiotherapy for Chronic Low Back Pain
abstract: A non-inferiority RCT (n=488 adults across 4 countries) compared telemedicine physiotherapy to face-to-face care for chronic low back pain. Mean NRS pain reduction was identical in both groups (-2.4 points) and within the 1.0-point non-inferiority margin. Patient satisfaction was marginally higher in the in-person group.

title: Social Media Use and Political Polarisation
abstract: A longitudinal survey study of 3,600 US adults (2018-2022) examined the relationship between Facebook and Twitter usage and measures of political affective polarisation. High social media use was associated with greater polarisation (β = 0.18, p<0.001) even after controlling for baseline attitudes. Causal mechanisms remain unclear.

title: School-Based Nutrition Education and Childhood Obesity
abstract: A systematic review and meta-analysis of 34 RCTs (n=28,430 children aged 5-18) found that school nutrition education reduced BMI z-score by -0.11 (95% CI -0.19 to -0.04). Effects were larger in primary-school children and programmes that involved parents. Fruit and vegetable intake outcomes showed stronger effects than anthropometric measures.
